message:
bd sync: close beads_rust-owu6 benchmark harness

Closes beads_rust-owu6: Benchmark harness implementation complete.

Added to tests/common/scenarios.rs:
- measure_peak_rss(): Linux VmHWM measurement
- measure_io_sizes(): DB and JSONL file size measurement
- compute_statistics(): median/mean/stddev/CV computation
- BenchmarkRunner: warmup + measured iterations with artifact logging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"beads_rust-gs0","title":"Phase 5: Polish & Conformance - Production Readiness","description":"# Phase 5: Polish & Conformance

## Goals
Deliver a production-ready `br` with **classic bd parity**, comprehensive testing, and ergonomic utility commands that do not expand scope beyond the three planning docs.

## Deliverables (children)
### Utility/Inspection Commands
- `doctor` (read-only diagnostics; no auto-fixes)
- `info`, `where`, `version`
- `stats/status`, `count`, `stale`, `orphans`
- `defer` / `undefer`
- `epic status` + `close-eligible`
- `graph` (deps visualization)
- `audit` (interactions.jsonl)
- `history` (.br_history) + `changelog`
- `q` (quick capture)
- `lint` (template/config validation)
- `saved queries` (save/run/list/delete)
- CSV export for list/export

### Test & QA Infrastructure
- Unit test infra and specs
- Snapshot/golden tests for human output
- E2E integration tests (CLI workflows)
- Conformance harness (bd vs br JSON parity)
- CI pipeline (fmt + clippy + tests)

### UX & Performance Polish
- Color output (opt-in or auto)
- Progress indicators for long ops
- Shell completions
- Performance benchmarks (startup + common paths)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conformance suite green; JSON parity with bd for classic commands.
- Utility commands are stable, documented, and tested.
- CI runs `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, and all tests.
- Performance baselines recorded; regressions detectable.

## Notes
- No daemon, no git hooks, no auto-git behavior.
- Utility commands must be read-only unless explicitly documented.

 {"id":"beads_rust-gu7b","title":"Code coverage reporting with tarpaulin","description":"# Code Coverage Reporting

## Overview
Configure cargo-tarpaulin for coverage reporting and CI integration.

## Installation
```bash
cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
```

## Configuration

### tarpaulin.toml
```toml
[coverage]
ignore-tests = true
follow-exec = true
timeout = "300s"
out = ["Html", "Lcov", "Json"]
output-dir = "coverage"

[coverage.run]
jobs = 4

[coverage.exclude]
# Exclude benchmarks and examples
paths = ["benches/*", "examples/*"]

# Exclude test helpers
test-patterns = ["test_helpers::*"]
```

## Coverage Targets

### Module Coverage Goals
| Module | Target | Priority |
|--------|--------|----------|
| src/storage/ | 90%+ | Critical - core data layer |
| src/sync/ | 85%+ | Critical - data integrity |
| src/cli/commands/ | 80%+ | High - user-facing |
| src/model/ | 85%+ | High - domain logic |
| src/format/ | 75%+ | Medium - output formatting |
| src/util/ | 70%+ | Medium - helpers |

### Overall Goals
- Minimum threshold: 70%
- Target: 85%+
- Stretch: 90%+

## Logging Requirements

### Coverage Run Logging
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# coverage.sh - Run with logging

echo "coverage_run_start: $(date -Iseconds)"
echo "coverage_config: ignore_tests=true output_dir=coverage"

# Run tarpaulin with verbose output
cargo tarpaulin --out Html --out Lcov --out Json 2>&1 | tee coverage/tarpaulin.log

# Parse and log results
COVERAGE=$(jq '.coverage_percent' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)
LINES_COVERED=$(jq '.covered_lines' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)
TOTAL_LINES=$(jq '.total_lines' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json)

echo "coverage_result: percent=${COVERAGE} covered=${LINES_COVERED} total=${TOTAL_LINES}"

# Per-module breakdown
echo "coverage_module_breakdown:"
jq -r '.files[] | "  \(.path): \(.coverage)%"' coverage/tarpaulin-report.json

echo "coverage_run_end: $(date -Iseconds)"
```

### CI Integration Logging
```yaml
# .github/workflows/coverage.yml
- name: Generate coverage
  run: |
    echo "::group::Coverage Generation"
    cargo tarpaulin --out Lcov --out Json 2>&1 | tee coverage.log
    echo "::endgroup::"
    
    # Log summary
    COVERAGE=$(jq '.coverage_percent' tarpaulin-report.json)
    echo "coverage_summary: ${COVERAGE}%"
    echo "::set-output name=coverage::${COVERAGE}"
    
    # Fail if below threshold
    if (( $(echo "$COVERAGE < 70" | bc -l) )); then
      echo "::error::Coverage ${COVERAGE}% below minimum 70%"
      exit 1
    fi

- name: Upload to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
  with:
    files: lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
    verbose: true
```

### Per-Module Coverage Logging
```rust
// In test harness, log coverage per module
#[cfg(test)]
mod coverage_logging {
    use std::process::Command;
    
    fn log_module_coverage(module: &str) {
        let output = Command::new("cargo")
            .args(["tarpaulin", "--out", "Json", "--packages", module])
            .output()
            .expect("tarpaulin failed");
        
        if output.status.success() {
            let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
            let coverage = json["coverage_percent"].as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
            println!("coverage_module: {}={:.1}%", module, coverage);
        }
    }
}
```

## Usage
```bash
# Generate coverage report
cargo tarpaulin --out Html

# View report
open coverage/tarpaulin-report.html

# Quick coverage check
cargo tarpaulin --out Stdout --skip-clean

# Module-specific coverage
cargo tarpaulin --packages beads_rust --out Html -- --test storage
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tarpaulin.toml configured with proper exclusions
- [ ] Coverage report generates locally
- [ ] CI workflow for coverage with threshold check
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Per-module breakdown logging
- [ ] Minimum 70% coverage enforced
- [ ] HTML + Lcov + JSON outputs generated

 {"id":"beads_rust-gxr9","title":"E2E tests: comments command","description":"# E2E Tests for `comments` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br comments add <issue_id> <text>` - Add comment to issue
- `br comments list <issue_id>` - List comments on issue
- `br comments --json` - JSON output mode

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Add single comment, verify in list
2. Add multiple comments, verify order (newest last)
3. List comments with --json, validate structure
4. Add comment to issue with existing comments

### Error Cases
5. Add comment to non-existent issue → error
6. Add empty comment → error or rejection
7. List comments on issue with no comments → empty list

### Edge Cases
8. Comment with special characters (quotes, newlines, unicode)
9. Very long comment (near limits)
10. Comment on closed issue (should work)

## Test Infrastructure
- Use tests/common/cli.rs BrWorkspace
- Capture stdout/stderr with logging
- Assert JSON shapes match expected
- Verify comment persists in DB and JSONL

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_comments.rs
- [ ] 10+ test functions covering above cases
- [ ] Tests use real storage, no mocks
- [ ] Rich tracing logs for debugging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperLake","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:25:40.126803216-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:13:12.96050997-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T11:13:12.96050997-05:00","close_reason":"E2E comments tests fully implemented: tests/e2e_comments.rs has 13 comprehensive tests covering comments command functionality.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-gxr9","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent_child","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:27:48.638371782-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-h2c","title":"Audit events: insertion rules + retrieval ordering","description":"# Audit Events (events table)

## Purpose
Implement the audit/event model exactly as classic bd: every mutation writes an event row inside the same transaction, and events are **local DB only** (never exported to JSONL). This bead defines event schema, insertion rules, and retrieval ordering.

## Schema (SQLite)
- `events` table: `id` (autoinc), `issue_id`, `event_type`, `actor`, `old_value`, `new_value`, `comment`, `created_at`.
- Indexes: `events.issue_id`, `events.event_type`, `events.created_at`, `events.actor` (see schema bead).

## Event Types (classic)
- `created`
- `updated`
- `status_changed`
- `closed`
- `reopened`
- `commented`
- `dependency_added` / `dependency_removed`
- `label_added` / `label_removed`
- `deleted` (tombstone) / `restored` (if restore supported)
- `compacted` (schema includes compaction fields even if compaction excluded)

## Insertion Rules (must be atomic with mutation)
- **CreateIssue**: emit `created` with actor.
- **UpdateIssue**: emit:
  - `status_changed` for non-terminal status transitions.
  - `closed` when status becomes `closed`.
  - `reopened` when moving from `closed` to `open`.
  - `updated` for other field changes.
- **CloseIssue**: `closed` with `comment` = close reason.
- **ReopenIssue**: `reopened` (and optionally a comment if `--reason` adds a comment).
- **DeleteIssue**: `deleted` with delete reason.
- **RestoreIssue** (if supported): `restored`.
- **Dep add/remove**: `dependency_added` / `dependency_removed` with comment like:
  - `Added dependency: <issue> <type> <depends_on>`
  - `Removed dependency on <depends_on>`
- **Label add/remove**: `label_added` / `label_removed`.
- **Comment add**: `commented` with `comment` text.

## Retrieval Semantics
- `GetEvents(issue_id, limit)` returns **newest first** (created_at DESC).
- Events are **not** exported to JSONL.
- CLI should only expose events via `show --events` (if implemented) or similar.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every mutation path inserts exactly one appropriate event row.
- Events are created **within the same transaction** as the mutation + dirty mark.
- `GetEvents` ordering is `created_at DESC`.
- JSONL export/import ignores events.

## Tests
- Unit tests for each mutation path verifying event_type + actor + timestamps.
- Ordering test for `GetEvents` (DESC).
- Integration test: create/update/close/dep/label/comment produce expected event sequence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:05:22.134434351Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:47:59.406508805Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:47:59.406508805Z","close_reason":"Implemented audit events module: schema, insertion functions, GetEvents with DESC ordering, 13 unit tests passing"}

 {"id":"beads_rust-hdc0","title":"Golden text snapshot system (color/whitespace normalization)","description":"Build a stable snapshot system for human-readable CLI output.

Scope
- Capture text output for key commands (list/show/ready/blocked/stats/etc).
- Normalize color codes, line endings, and whitespace for portability.
- Provide diff output that highlights semantic changes.

Acceptance
- Snapshots are deterministic across platforms.
- Failures show clear before/after diffs in artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T22:49:20.87452829-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T22:49:20.87452829-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-hdc0","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T22:49:37.434223862-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-hdc0","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ir0t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T22:49:45.335008833-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-hdc0","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-r23m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T22:49:45.384925879-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
